root_causes.description: AST and DI resolver drift created conflicting dependency interpretations that amplified downstream topology instability.
Cycle policy violations introduced unstable semantic paths and increased propagation risk across connected components.
Missing dependency edges in DI wiring broke upstream dependency chains and produced downstream unreachable/orphan symptoms.
Unresolved or malformed graph references corrupted structural integrity and propagated downstream reachability symptoms.

sequence.event: Dependency resolution missed required DI edges
Resolver disagreement confirmed missing/shifted dependency wiring
AST and DI resolver outputs diverged
Reachability behavior shifted under inconsistent edges

system_anomalies.description: Nodes with no effective connectivity surfaced as downstream symptoms of upstream dependency or structural breaks.
Cycle-policy violations increase semantic instability and can amplify downstream propagation.
High-severity resolver disagreements indicate dependency interpretation instability.
DI-linked nodes are missing dependency edges, indicating upstream dependency breakpoints.
